perturbation.original_text: Upon the terms and subject to the conditions of this Agreement, Developer hereby grants to Distributor an exclusive, non-transferable fight and license to market and distribute the Products in the Territory.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon the terms and subject to the conditions of this Agreement, Developer hereby grants to Distributor a non-exclusive, non-transferable fight and license to market and distribute the Products in the Territory.
perturbation.explanation: This change makes the distribution license non-exclusive, directly contradicting the initial statement in section 1.1 that the license is exclusive. This creates an ambiguity regarding the level of exclusivity the Distributor has. This in-text contradiction will significantly impact the agreement, potentially allowing Developer to license the product to other distributors in the same territory. This contradicts with the term EXCLUSIVE DISTRIBUTOR AGREEMENT being made on the heading. Also there is a contradiction with definition of Territory in section 1.2 where, Distributor may market and distribute the Products solely within the Territory
perturbation.contradicted_text: Distributor may market and distribute the Products solely within the Territory. Distributor shall not have the right to establish third-party agreements for the license, sale, installation and/or support of the Products in the Territory or elsewhere, without the prior written approval of Developer, which approval may be withheld for any reason.
perturbation.justification: The original text grants an 'exclusive' license, whereas the changed text makes it 'non-exclusive'. This directly contradicts the statement 'Distributor may market and distribute the Products solely within the Territory' because a non-exclusive license means other distributors could also market and distribute the products within the same territory, breaking the exclusivity originally implied.
